title: The Beef Carcase Classification scheme: guidance
body: " View this collection document collection in Cymraeg (Welsh)   To operate an abattoir in the UK you must get approval from the Food Standards Agency.  You must also register with the Beef Carcase Classification (BCC) scheme if all of the following apply:   your business is in England or Wales you slaughter 150 (or more) bovine animals aged 8 months or over per week.   The number of animals per week is calculated on a rolling annual average basis.  Under the BCC scheme, you must classify carcases according to rules which cover:   carcase dressing, weighing and labelling/marking communicating the classification results to the supplier record keeping inspections, enforcement and penalties licensing.    If you slaughter fewer than 150 bovine animals (aged 8 months or over) per week If you slaughter fewer than 150 bovine animals (aged 8 months or over) per week but want to classify beef carcases you can register with the BCC scheme as a small-scale operator.  If you do this you need to meet all the scheme requirements You can only use the European Union (EU) classification scales if you are registered with the scheme. Only a licensed classifier can carry out classification.   Read old guidance on the National Archives website  Relevant regulations  The Carcase Classification and Price Reporting (England) Regulations 2018 (SI 2018/1164)  The Carcase Classification and Price Reporting (Wales) Regulations 2018 (S.I. 2018 No. 1215(W0248)  Regulation EUR 2013/1308  Regulation EUR 2017/1182 and EUR 2017/1184  "
